denominators.definition: Generated rows for each finalized model/configuration/seed run.
denominators.name: generated feasibility

denominators.aggregation: Arithmetic mean of the four per-seed fractions; material and geometry categories are non-exclusive.
denominators.definition: Generated rows for each tuned or untuned seed; material and geometry categories are non-exclusive.
denominators.name: representation issue rates

manifest.reason: A manifest cannot contain a stable hash of itself.

representation_issue_accounting.aggregation: Arithmetic mean of the four per-seed fractions; material and geometry categories are non-exclusive.

notes: The stable output path replaces the superseded dense-v2 main figure in the manuscript.
Panel headers and evidence regions are edge-to-edge tinted bands clipped to the rounded panel boundary and separated from the body by straight divider lines.
Framed subplot and module headers use the same edge-to-edge clipped fill, with dividers drawn to the outward-padded frame extents.
Panel C uses four uniformly sized and spaced post-processing boxes with centered labels and no step numbers.
The first Panel C box identifies the sampled design prescription entering post-processing.
Panel headers omit stage labels and render each panel title in its accent color.
The exported figure contains only the four panels; the global title, subtitle, and generation badge are omitted.
Panel header and footer text is set to 12.5 pt and positioned at the geometric center of each colored band.
Panels B-D align footer values to the panel-badge left edge and footer descriptions to the mirrored right inset.
Subplot and framed-module titles use the existing 9 pt major typography level.
Panel B representation mappings use 9 pt single-line text with left-shifted arrows and a wider transformed-value column.
Panel B right-side benefit and issue-reduction rows use the same 9 pt typography as the representation mappings.
The standardized typography hierarchy is 12.5 pt panel bands, 10 pt titles and emphasized rows, 9 pt body text, and 8 pt annotations.
Panel A shows light major grid lines for surface-count shares and a labeled colorbar for the synthetic performance-density field.
Panels A and C align the outer edges of their paired evidence modules with the flow-chart bounds while preserving the existing center gap.
Panel A centers the performance axes and density colorbar as one visual group within the right evidence module.
Panel A density colors use log(1 + count), while the colorbar reports raw synthetic sample counts per bin at 1, 10, 100, and 1,000.
Panels B and C use the LaTeX right-arrow glyph for feasibility transitions in their evidence strips.
Panel B uses the same LaTeX right-arrow glyph for representation mappings and issue-rate reductions.
Panel A recenters the performance group after accounting for the full raw-count colorbar label width.
Panel C derives its lower-module outer bounds from the flow-chart bounds so both rows align exactly at each edge.
All plot legends use the standardized 8 pt regular Times text in the shared muted-gray color.
Panel C increases horizontal spacing between the three ray-tracing legend entries while retaining their centered layout.
Panel B presents each issue-rate reduction in a separate bordered green card with visible inter-card spacing.
The SVG export is normalized to remove generated trailing whitespace before output hashes are recorded.
Panel D patent and generated lens assemblies are rendered from recorded physical prescription rows.
Panel A reads the saved all-row density grid and exact empirical fronts; it does not recompute or subsample Pareto metrics.
Panel C rays are traced by the production ray engine and terminate at its computed image plane.
Panel C heatmap reads the finalized four-run paired field summary and reports mean within-run median percent reductions.
Panel B material and geometry issue rates are non-exclusive four-seed means from the publication submission manifests.
Panel B ray-tracing error rates are CodeV error rows divided by generated rows in the finalized Section 6.2 per-run table.
Panel D radar values use the finalized all-row four-seed statistical summary and full-data feasible fractions; each axis retains its documented raw range and is oriented so outward is better.
Panel D condition-matched examples use the deterministic 10% F-number and EPD selection with no visual screening.

Panel D dumbbells use metric-specific logarithmic axes beneath the generated design columns; the widened space beneath the patent card contains complete metric names and log ranges. Green and red segments indicate lower and higher generated values relative to the patent reference.
Panel D uses a visual legend with colored blocks for improved-versus-patent and degraded-versus-patent values and the plotted orange star for the patent reference.
The patent-reference star is enlarged consistently in the design card, dumbbells, and visual key for manuscript-scale visibility.
All panel text is normalized to four documented font sizes; the global figure title remains outside this panel hierarchy.
All figure text is rendered by LaTeX using the manuscript's PSNFSS Times family; text positions are unchanged from the standardized-typography source.
All text in panel header bands and bottom evidence strips is set to 11 pt; interior panel typography is unchanged.
Panel B and Panel C evidence labels use the shared 'Feasibility improvement through ...' construction; Panel D omits the redundant secondary metric-summary line.
No model training, sampling, metric recomputation, or candidate reselection is performed.

panel_a_pareto.density_colorbar: logarithmic color mapping labeled with raw synthetic sample counts per 2D histogram bin

display.pareto_rendering: Exact empirical lower-left fronts computed from all valid rows before display limits.
display.patent_rendering: All visible patent rows are shown as scatter points.
display.synthetic_rendering: Filled contours of log(1 + exact two-dimensional histogram count).

evaluation_scope.field_aggregation: median across all five CodeV field positions
evaluation_scope.invalid_row_policy: Rows with nonpositive or nonfinite Airy diameter, nonpositive or nonfinite normalized SPO67 or EE90, or nonfinite distortion are rejected.

metric_definitions.median_ee90_per_airy_diameter: Per-row median across ee90_00 through ee90_04 after each field value is divided by the row's Airy diameter.
metric_definitions.median_spo67_per_airy_diameter: Per-row median across spo67_00 through spo67_04 after each field value is divided by the row's Airy diameter.
metric_definitions.pareto_direction: Both metrics are minimized; lower-left is better.

paired_reduction_heatmap.value: mean across runs of the within-run median paired percent reduction

ray_visualization.display_rule: Only paths whose final recorded point is on the computed image plane are shown.

panel_d.performance_direction: lower is better for all five dumbbell metrics
panel_d.performance_layout: expanded metric-label and log-range column beneath the patent card; dumbbells aligned beneath generated design columns

legacy_evidence.purpose: Curated dense 2x2 main publication figure with the finalized four-stage narrative and optical-performance Pareto evidence.

limitations: The figure is a curated summary and does not replace the section-level tables and manifests.
Displayed prescriptions are deterministic examples selected by the recorded 10% condition-matching procedure.
PDF and SVG byte hashes can vary because Matplotlib embeds generation metadata and generated element identifiers; visual regression is authoritative for those formats.

metrics.definition: Dataset scale, normalized optical performance, feasibility, issue-rate reductions, statistical fidelity/diversity/novelty, and condition-matched prescription evidence.
metrics.direction: Metric-specific: higher is better for feasibility and outward-oriented radar axes; lower is better for normalized optical errors and issue rates.
metrics.ideal: Metric-specific; outward is better on the radar axes and lower is better for normalized optical errors.
metrics.name: main figure evidence

preprocessing: Use the stored surface-count table without regrouping.
Use the saved all-row synthetic density grid and exact empirical Pareto fronts.
Normalize SPO67 and EE90 by the recorded Airy references as defined by the Pareto manifest.
Use the finalized four-run paired field summary for post-processing reductions.
Render the recorded physical prescriptions and production ray traces without candidate reselection.

panel_b_representation_issue_rates.aggregation: Arithmetic mean of the four per-seed fractions; material and geometry categories are non-exclusive.

before_after.name: panel_d_condition_matched_selection
before_after.selection: Low F/#, high EPD slots A, B, and C

before_after.name: panel_d_patent_metrics
before_after.selection: Low F/#, high EPD patent anchor

population_evidence.scope: All finalized population plot and summary inputs in Panels A-C and the Panel D full-data model benchmark

selection.reference_split: Finalized publication artifacts and selected-design records

skipped_checks: No model training, sampling, metric recomputation, or candidate reselection is performed.
The existence-gated Panel D selection manifest is hashed but not parsed by the inherited renderer.
